title: JobPlan
description: A reusable, version-controlled maintenance job plan — the WHAT of a piece of work (tasks, labour, parts, readings, safety). Standalone: references to assets, parts and skills are held as free text/keys and resolve to real records only if/when those modules exist. One JSON document = one job plan, embedding all its versions.

assetClass.description: Asset class this plan belongs to (e.g. UPS, Generator, Chiller). Plans roll up under their class in the gallery, alphabetically.

assetModel.description: Optional asset model within the class (e.g. Schneider Galaxy VX). When present, plans sub-group by model under the class.

rev.description: Optimistic-concurrency revision; bumped on every save.

activeVersion.description: Version number currently in the 'active' state, if any. At most one version is active.

versions.description: All versions, retained forever. A version is immutable once it leaves 'draft'.

lifecycleState.description: draft -> approved -> active -> superseded -> retired. Only 'draft' is editable.

frequencies.description: Work-order generation frequencies this plan can drop at (daily, weekly, monthly, n-monthly, yearly, n-yearly). Each task is mapped to the frequencies it belongs to via task.frequencyIds — a task x frequency matrix. When a frequency falls due, a work order is generated containing the tasks mapped to it.

parameters.description: Named values the plan is parameterised on (e.g. operating voltage). Resolve from an asset/model template in future; entered manually for now.

responseType.description: Expected response captured when the task is performed.

unit.description: Unit for a numeric reading, e.g. V, A, mOhm.

acceptableRange.description: [low, high] acceptance bounds for a numeric reading.

frequencyIds.description: Ids of the frequencies this task drops at (its ticked cells in the task x frequency matrix). A task may belong to several frequencies.

frequency.description: A recurrence column in the matrix. unit x interval expresses daily (day/1), weekly (week/1), monthly (month/1), n-monthly (month/n), yearly (year/1) and n-yearly (year/n).

label.description: Display label, e.g. 'Quarterly'; derivable from unit/interval.

basis.description: Fixed calendar dates vs recurring from last completion.

partRef.description: Future reference to a Part; free text for now.

source.description: Where the value resolves from once assets/models exist.
